summary: Added a repo-root `resync.sh` helper script for non-destructively syncing a local fork/branch with `doldecomp/master`. The script commits any working tree changes, fetches upstream master, soft-resets to `FETCH_HEAD`, commits the resulting merge state, and force-pushes the branch. The PR had no review discussion, so intent is drawn from the PR body and script contents.
what_changed: Created a new `resync.sh` shell script at the repository root.
Defined a `safe_commit()` helper that only runs `git commit -m "$1"` when either unstaged or staged diffs exist.
The script stages all local changes with `git add --all` and commits them as `Automatically commit working tree changes` if needed.
The script uses `git reset --soft FETCH_HEAD` to move the branch to upstream master while preserving local changes in the index/worktree for a follow-up commit.
The script commits the post-reset state as `Automatically merge with doldecomp/master` if there are changes, then runs `git push --force`.
smart_moves: Used a small `safe_commit` wrapper to avoid failing on empty commits when there are no local or staged changes.
Used `git reset --soft FETCH_HEAD` rather than a destructive hard reset, matching the PR body's claim that the sync is non-destructive.
Made the upstream target explicit by fetching from `https://github.com/doldecomp/melee.git master`, avoiding dependence on a user's remote naming convention.
Automated the common fork-resync workflow into a repeatable script for contributors.
decomp_lessons: Workflow helpers can reduce friction for contributors keeping long-lived decomp branches synchronized with `doldecomp/master`.

When adding repository scripts that alter git history, make the behavior explicit in commit messages and PR description because future users need to understand that force-push is part of the workflow.
For non-destructive synchronization tooling, prefer preserving local changes through an automatic commit or soft reset rather than requiring users to manually stash or discard work.
naming_conventions: `resync.sh` names the script by its user-facing action: resyncing a branch/fork with upstream.
`safe_commit` describes a conditional commit helper that commits only when there is something to commit.
Automatic commit messages are explicit and prefixed with `Automatically`, e.g. `Automatically commit working tree changes` and `Automatically merge with doldecomp/master`.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the PR dump.
No reviewer-requested naming, matching, or structural changes were available as evidence.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that modify or remove `resync.sh` to see whether force-push or soft-reset behavior was revised.
Search contributor documentation for references to `resync.sh` or fork resync workflow.
Look for issues or PR comments mentioning problems with `git push --force` in the resync workflow.
